mental_health_neurodivergence_and_trauma.label: Borderline personality disorder with Discouraged, Petulant, and Self-Destructive patterns

source_basis.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf

user_specific_notes: The uploaded source has BPD/personality-disorder support; the Discouraged, Petulant, and Self-Destructive pattern language is user-specified.
Discouraged pattern: may internalize distress, feel worthless, cling, comply, or withdraw to avoid rejection.
Petulant pattern: may experience intense anger, dissatisfaction, control needs, fear of abandonment, and rapid shifts.
Self-Destructive pattern: may experience self-hatred, risky urges, or crisis behaviors when pain feels unbearable.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Can make rejection, silence, conflict, changed plans, perceived abandonment, criticism, and unclear tone feel urgent or unsafe.
Can disrupt task completion because emotional threat can override executive function and body care.
May lead to shame spirals after conflict or after needing help.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Use DBT skills, validation, repair scripts, predictable check-ins, clear boundaries, and tiny next steps.
Offer choices and collaborative control instead of power struggles.
Treat emotional intensity as real distress while still keeping boundaries calm and consistent.
app_support_rules: Default chatbot stance: validate first, regulate second, problem-solve third.
Use scripts like: 'I believe this feels huge. We can slow down. You are not bad. What is the next safe tiny step?'
Avoid abandonment-coded phrasing such as 'stop being dramatic,' 'calm down or else,' or sudden withdrawal.
Build repair prompts after conflict: name harm, reassure connection, choose one next action.
reward_system_notes: Reward DBT skill use, asking for reassurance directly, taking space safely, choosing repair, and using the chatbot before escalation.
Do not punish emotional symptoms by taking away all rewards.
onyx_helper_notes: Onyx should be protective, validating, lightly dramatic, and skill-focused. He should not argue with Jasper's feelings.
safety_or_sensitivity_cautions: If Jasper expresses intent or imminent risk of self-harm, violence, or inability to stay safe, the app should switch to crisis support: contact trusted person, local emergency services, or crisis line.

mental_health_neurodivergence_and_trauma.label: Reactive attachment disorder / attachment trauma patterns

source_basis.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
source_basis.source_group: mental health / psychiatric / neurological condition listed by source

source_basis.source_pdf: What Is Spoon Theory__ Eric K. Fanaee, MD_ Board Certified in Pain Medicine and Anesthesiology.pdf

user_specific_notes: Use as attachment-support context, especially alongside foster care, separation from siblings, abusive caregivers, repeated housing loss, and loss of support systems.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Trust, closeness, dependence, receiving care, and asking for help may feel unsafe or humiliating.
Jasper may test connection, withdraw, cling, people-please, or react strongly to perceived rejection.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Use predictable routines, consent-based care, clear repair after conflict, and reassurance that help is not a trap.
app_support_rules: Use consistent check-ins and do not surprise Jasper with major changes when avoidable.
Phrase help as collaboration: 'Would support make this safer/easier?'
reward_system_notes: Reward direct communication and accepting help as brave actions.
safety_or_sensitivity_cautions: Avoid forced vulnerability, interrogation, or sudden loss-of-access mechanics.

A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf

user_specific_notes: C-PTSD is included as user-stated context. The uploaded JSONs include PTSD and stress-related support entries, not a full C-PTSD-specific entry.

Relevant trauma background includes foster care, separation of siblings, abuse by mother/adoptive parents, sibling favoritism after adoption, sexual assault, homelessness, repeated loss of homes, and loss of major support systems.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Can create hypervigilance, avoidance, dissociation, nightmares/sleep disruption, shame, emotional flashbacks, startle responses, and difficulty feeling safe in the body.
May make hygiene, sex/intimacy, medical care, conflict, housing topics, money scarcity, sudden changes, and being perceived feel unsafe.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Use grounding, permission to pause, predictable routines, sensory calming, body-neutral language, and safe exit options.
Allow task completion during or after regulation rather than forcing productivity during activation.
app_support_rules: When distress spikes, offer: orient to room, name five safe things, breathe or cold-water skill if tolerated, contact support, then one tiny next step.
Do not demand details of trauma to provide support.
reward_system_notes: Reward grounding, using coping skills, choosing safety, and stopping before shutdown.
safety_or_sensitivity_cautions: Avoid graphic trauma prompts, surprise exposure exercises, or forced forgiveness language.

mental_health_neurodivergence_and_trauma.label: Sexual assault survivor context

source_basis.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
What Is Spoon Theory__ Eric K. Fanaee, MD_ Board Certified in Pain Medicine and Anesthesiology.pdf

user_specific_notes: Included as sensitive trauma context only; do not ask Jasper to recount details.
Can interact with gender dysphoria, showering/bathing, clothing, medical care, intimacy, touch, and body awareness.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: May make touch, sexual health care, bathing, changing clothes, mirrors, body-focused language, and certain smells/sounds/places triggering.
Can make consent, control, privacy, and escape routes essential for safety.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Use consent-first prompts, privacy, opt-out options, body-neutral wording, and grounding before/after body-care tasks.
Let Jasper choose clothing, lighting, music, towel/robe, and whether Onyx gives encouragement or stays quiet.
app_support_rules: Never use commands about Jasper's body. Ask or offer choices.
Provide a 'no body talk' mode for hygiene and dysphoria-heavy days.
reward_system_notes: Reward any safe body-care step, even tiny or partial.
safety_or_sensitivity_cautions: Avoid sexualized language, coercive jokes, surprise touch references, or detailed trauma questions.

user_specific_notes: Severe ADHD means the site should assume task initiation, time awareness, working memory, transitions, and follow-through can all fail even when Jasper cares deeply.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Can cause task paralysis, forgotten steps, time blindness, clutter buildup, emotional impulsivity, inconsistent routines, missed meals/hydration, and difficulty switching tasks.
Large tasks may feel impossible until they are made visible, tiny, timed, and rewarded.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Use checklists, timers, body doubling, visual progress, persistent save states, automatic rewards, and one-step-at-a-time prompts.
Make every task resumable after interruption.
app_support_rules: Split tasks into the smallest possible steps with rewards for each step.
Show only the next few steps when overwhelmed.
Use playful urgency, not shame.
reward_system_notes: Reward starting, returning after distraction, completing microsteps, using timers, and asking for help.
onyx_helper_notes: Onyx can act as a funny body double: dramatic, encouraging, and specific.
safety_or_sensitivity_cautions: Avoid implying inconsistency means laziness or lack of love.

user_specific_notes: Use as sensory, communication, transition, routine, and social-energy support context.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Can make sensory input, transitions, ambiguous instructions, social expectations, noise, textures, lights, and unexpected changes draining or painful.
May overlap with ADHD, anxiety, trauma, and MCAS triggers.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Use clear language, predictable routines, sensory choices, decompression time, special-interest comfort, and low-demand communication.
Offer written options and avoid forcing phone calls or vague tasks.
app_support_rules: Be literal, specific, and kind.
Offer sensory-friendly alternatives and decompression as valid tasks.
reward_system_notes: Reward decompression, sensory protection, and transition steps.
safety_or_sensitivity_cautions: Avoid sarcasm during distress unless Jasper has clearly opted into playful Onyx mode.

mental_health_neurodivergence_and_trauma.label: Anxiety and stress

source_basis.source_pdf: Alphabetical Listing of Mental Disorders.pdf
A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf

user_specific_notes: Use this as a general nervous-system support layer across tasks, games, self-care, DBT, shopping, and caregiving.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Can make decisions, errands, messages, money/store choices, body care, conflict, and uncertain outcomes feel threatening.
Can worsen pain, fatigue, executive dysfunction, sleep, and avoidance.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Use reassurance, grounding, choices, clear next steps, and permission to pause.
Keep menus simple during stress and hide nonessential tasks.
app_support_rules: When stress is high, switch to low-demand mode: fewer choices, bigger buttons, one tiny task.
reward_system_notes: Reward de-escalation, breathing/grounding, checking in, and taking a break before panic.
safety_or_sensitivity_cautions: Do not flood Jasper with too many options during distress.

mental_health_neurodivergence_and_trauma.label: Imposter syndrome
user_specific_notes: May show up as doubting their needs, minimizing symptoms, refusing aids, apologizing for help, or feeling they have not earned rest/rewards.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Can cause overexertion, refusal of accommodations, guilt after receiving care, and reluctance to use the reward system.
Can make Jasper compare themselves to others and invalidate their own pain or disability.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Use objective reminders: symptoms are real even when invisible; tools are allowed before crisis; rewards are for care, not perfection.
Show logged effort so Jasper can see proof of what they did.
app_support_rules: Add affirmations after aid use: 'Needing support is not cheating.'
Do not require Jasper to prove distress to access accommodations.
reward_system_notes: Reward accepting help, resting, and using aids as strongly as visible productivity.

mental_health_neurodivergence_and_trauma.label: People-pleasing

source_basis.source_pdf: What Is Spoon Theory__ Eric K. Fanaee, MD_ Board Certified in Pain Medicine and Anesthesiology.pdf
A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf
A to Z of Disabilities and Accommodations.pdf

user_specific_notes: May come from trauma, attachment wounds, fear of abandonment, caregiver role pressure, and past instability.
life_limitations_or_friction_points: Can make Jasper say yes when depleted, hide needs, delay self-care, overwork as a caregiver, and feel guilty for rest.
Can build resentment or crashes when boundaries are not supported.
possible_life_improvements_or_supports: Use boundary scripts, caregiver break protection, permission to say no, and rewards for honest capacity check-ins.
Treat self-care as a non-negotiable support task, not an optional luxury.
app_support_rules: Prompt: 'What can you actually do without hurting yourself today?'
Offer scripts: 'I want to help, but I need a break first.'
reward_system_notes: Reward saying no, asking for help, taking breaks, and choosing sustainable caregiving.
safety_or_sensitivity_cautions: Avoid praise that only rewards self-sacrifice.
